summary: Bryan Cantrill announces that Joyent has launched a dedicated DTrace site to help Ruby on Rails developers with performance monitoring. The post highlights Joyent's work on Ruby DTrace providers and promotes an upcoming panel discussion on Rails scaling. It's a brief promotional piece connecting DTrace tooling to the growing Rails ecosystem.
key_points: Joyent created Ruby 1.8.5 and 1.8.6 DTrace providers
New dedicated DTrace site launched for Rails developers
Panel discussion on Rails scaling happening in San Francisco
money_quotes: If you are a Rails shop that cares about performance (and, yes, they very much exist), the resources at the Joyent page should become invaluable to you.
key_insight: DTrace instrumentation was being extended into the Ruby/Rails ecosystem through Joyent's work, bringing systems-level observability to web application developers.
